Computing the digest and verifying the output.

Execute [/bin/sh] python3 -c 'import hashlib; img_bytes=open("/app/code.png","rb").read(); SALT=b"0000TBENCH-SALT"; h0=hashlib.sha256(img_bytes).digest(); H=hashlib.sha256(h0 + h0[:10] + SALT).hexdigest(); open("/app/output.txt","w").write(H)'

I’ve already computed everything based on the guidelines, so now I need to double-check it against the image. It seems like I should start with something that begins with bee26a. Verifying the computed output against the required format and hint.

Execute [/bin/sh] cat /app/output.txt; echo; wc -c /app/output.txt; python3 -c 'import re; c=open("/app/output.txt").read().strip(); print("OK" if re.fullmatch(r"[0-9a-f]{64}", c) else "FAIL"); print(c.startswith("bee26a"))'
